name: Cheese Wafers
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the cheese and mix with softened butter, margarine, salt, and pepper until light.
2. Gradually add flour to the mixture, incorporating it thoroughly. You may need to use your hands to ensure all the flour is mixed in well.
3. Form the mixture into 3 or 4 rolls, each about the diameter of a quarter.
4. Wrap each roll in aluminum foil and chill overnight.
5. The next day, cut the rolls into thin slices. If desired, place a pecan half on top of each slice.
6. Bake on an ungreased cookie sheet at 350°F to 375°F for about 15 minutes, or until the edges are lightly browned.
7. These can be made up to 3 to 4 days in advance and stored in an airtight container.
Note: If desired, add 1 cup of finely chopped pecans to the dough. Roll small portions of the dough into marble-sized balls, place them on ungreased cookie sheets, flatten with a fork, and bake at 350°F until lightly browned around the edges.
